claim: Residual TD3 is more stable than Direct TD3
acceptance_rule: Residual and disturbance-aware residual TD3 should have lower failure_rate than Direct TD3 and no worse rotor_saturation_rate in the main scenarios
notes: After the force-point fix, 5000-step pilot has Direct TD3 failure_rate 1.0 in every main scenario. Conservative warm-start-retained DA-Residual selected models reduce failure_rate to 0.0 in standard/dust/thermal and 0.111 in wind/compound.

claim: Disturbance observation improves tracking under compound disturbances
acceptance_rule: Disturbance-aware residual TD3 should reduce compound steady_position_rmse relative to residual_td3 without disturbance observations under a matched conservative retention protocol; unseen is reported separately as out-of-distribution stress evidence
notes: Matched conservative no-disturbance-observation residual_td3 ablation completed. Main test: DA wind/compound steady RMSE 0.6914/0.6821 vs residual_td3 0.7528/0.7580, with the same failure_rate 0.111. Compound generalization also favors DA: 0.7511 vs 0.8644 at R=0.4,T=8 and 0.9039 vs 0.9580 at R=0.5,T=12. Unseen reverses this pattern: residual_td3 has failure_rate 0.667 and steady RMSE 0.9992/0.9387 vs DA failure_rate 0.778 and steady RMSE 1.2079/1.3708.

claim: Safety gating reduces saturation and abnormal actions
acceptance_rule: Safety-gated controller should reduce rotor_saturation_rate or action_smoothness without increasing standard-scene RMSE beyond the PID tolerance
notes: 5000-step fixed-physics pilot: gated DA-Residual has failure_rate 0 in standard/dust/thermal while no-gate fails in standard/thermal and has much larger action_smoothness. Conservative selected checkpoint keeps standard fallback exactly.

claim: The full method does not materially degrade PID in the standard environment
acceptance_rule: standard position_rmse of the full method <= 1.10 * PID position_rmse and failure_rate == 0
notes: Conservative selected multiseed standard scene: PID and DA-Residual both have position_rmse 0.3607, steady_rmse 0.3458, and failure_rate 0.0.

claim: The full method improves circular tracking under wind and compound disturbances relative to plain PID
acceptance_rule: Full method should beat PID on wind and compound failure_rate and steady_position_rmse
notes: Conservative multiseed selected models: wind failure_rate 0.111 and steady RMSE 0.6914 vs PID 0.333 and 0.9970; compound failure_rate 0.111 and steady RMSE 0.6821 vs PID 0.333 and 0.9537. It is still worse than PID-FF in wind/compound and worse than PID/PID-FF in thermal/dust.

claim: Unseen disturbance evaluation characterizes generalization rather than main-distribution performance
acceptance_rule: Report unseen results separately from the main test table; do not tune on unseen seeds
notes: Conservative multiseed selected models improve compound generalization relative to PID but remain weaker than PID-FF. Unseen failure_rate is 0.778 at R=0.4,T=8 and R=0.5,T=12; PID-FF remains stronger with failure_rate 0.333 in both tests.

claim: PID-FF imitation warm-start improves residual initialization but naive TD3 fine-tuning is unstable
acceptance_rule: Warm-start imitation should improve disturbed tracking without losing standard fallback; TD3 fine-tuning should preserve or improve the warm-started policy
notes: Naive warm-start final and 4000-step selected checkpoint degrade in the first run. Conservative retention plus validation selection picks different candidates by seed and improves wind/compound relative to PID, but seed2 final still fails one wind and one compound rollout.

claim: Conservative warm-start retention and checkpoint selection produce a bounded positive pilot result
acceptance_rule: Selected models should preserve standard PID fallback and improve wind/compound relative to plain PID while reporting PID-FF as stronger
notes: The gate=0.8 multiseed selected models satisfy the bounded main-distribution claim: exact standard fallback, lower wind/compound failure_rate than PID, and lower wind/compound steady RMSE than PID. Selected candidates differ by seed: model_1000_steps, warm_start, and final. Limitations: weaker than PID-FF, worse thermal/dust than PID, seed2 has one wind and one compound failure, and unseen generalization remains weak.
